perturbation.original_text: "Required Capital Contributions" means some combination of (a) one or more unrestricted cash contributions to the capital of one or more of the Borrowers made by the Investor, and/or (b) one or more Qualified Subordinated Loans made by the Investor to one or more of the Borrowers, in each case as required under Section 2 below, in any or all cases, in an aggregate amount not to exceed $5,000,000 during the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: "Required Capital Contributions" means a voluntary contribution, at the discretion of the Investor, to the capital of one or more of the Borrowers made by the Investor, and/or a standard loan made by the Investor to one or more of the Borrowers, in each case as potentially requested under Section 2 below, in any or all cases, in a suggested amount not to exceed $5,000,000 during the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines 'Required Capital Contributions' as an obligation to be fulfilled. The changed text makes it a 'voluntary contribution' at the discretion of the Investor. This contradicts the mandatory nature implied elsewhere in the agreement when discussing obligations and default scenarios tied to these contributions. The mandatory term Required Capital contribution is now a suggestion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Investor agrees to make, and the applicable Borrower(s) agree(s) to accept, Required Capital Contributions in respect of the applicable 12 Month Period (or shorter period prior to July 3, 2006, as applicable) in an amount sufficient to cause (via an addition to Adjusted EBITDAR in the amount of such Required Capital Contributions) the Borrowers to comply with Section 2 of Schedule 10.28 to the Financing Agreement as of the last day of each Fiscal Quarter. Notwithstanding the foregoing or any other provision of this Agreement to the contrary, the Agent acknowledges and agrees that the Investor's obligation to make Required Capital Contributions hereunder shall be limited to an aggregate amount of $5,000,000 during the term of this Agreement. The Agent further acknowledges and agrees that, regardless of whether other "Events of Default" (as defined therein) have occurred and are continuing under the Financing Agreement, in no event shall the Investor be obligated to make any Required Capital Contributions hereunder if the Borrowers have fully complied with Section 2 of Schedule 10.28 to the Financing Agreement as of the last day of the applicable Fiscal Quarter for the applicable 12 Month Period (or shorter period prior to July 3, 2006, as applicable).
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Required Capital Contributions' as mandatory, aligning with the contradicted text which states the Investor 'agrees to make' these contributions. The changed text redefines 'Required Capital Contributions' as 'voluntary', directly contradicting the Investor's agreed obligation in the contradicted text. The original text states that 'the Investor's obligation to make Required Capital Contributions', the changed text's new terminology causes a direct contradiction

perturbation.original_text: Specific Performance and Liquidated Damages. The Investor acknowledges and agrees that if a Maintenance Event of Default (as defined in Section 10 below) has occurred and is continuing hereunder, the Agent, for itself and on behalf of the Lenders, shall have the non-exclusive right to obtain specific performance of the obligation of the Investor to make the Required Capital Contributions.
perturbation.changed_text: Equitable Remedies. The Investor acknowledges and agrees that if a Maintenance Event of Default (as defined in Section 10 below) has occurred and is continuing hereunder, the Agent, for itself and on behalf of the Lenders, shall have the non-exclusive right to pursue equitable remedies related to the potential desire of the Investor to make any Capital Supplements.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Specific Performance of the obligation of the Investor to make Required Capital Contribution' to 'equitable remedies related to the potential desire of the Investor to make any Capital Supplements' introduces a contradiction. It replaces a clear, enforceable obligation with a vague reference to 'equitable remedies' connected to a discretionary act (desire to make Capital Supplements). This makes the enforcement much weaker.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any one or more of the following events shall constitute a "Maintenance Event of Default" under this Agreement: (a) if the Investor shall fail to pay, when and as due, any Required Capital Contribution and such failure shall continue for five (5) days; (b) if the Investor shall fail to perform any other of the Investor's obligations in this Agreement, and such failure shall continue for fifteen (15) days, (c) if any warranty or statement made or information provided 4 by the Investor in connection with this Agreement is untrue or misleading in any material respect on the date made; (d) if Investor shall, at any time, fail to have committed capital in an aggregate minimum amount equal to $5,000,000 less the sum of all Required Capital Contributions (if any) actually made by the Investor during the term of this Agreement, as determined based on the most recent information made available by the Investor to the Agent pursuant to Section 20 below; or (e) the Investor shall become insolvent or generally fail to pay, or admit in writing its inability to pay, its debts as they mature or apply for or consent to the appointment of a trustee or other custodian for its properties, or make a general assignment for the benefit of creditors, or any bankruptcy, reorganization, debt arrangement or other proceeding under any bankruptcy or insolvency law, is instituted by or against the Investor. Upon the occurrence of any Maintenance Event of Default hereunder, the Agent, for itself and on behalf of the Lenders, may exercise any of its rights and remedies available at law or in equity (including, without limitation, the rights and remedies described in Section 4 above). The Investor and the Borrowers each expressly acknowledge and agree that the occurrence of any Maintenance Event of Default hereunder shall constitute an "Event of Default" (as defined therein) under the Financing Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the enforceability of the Investor's obligation to make Required Capital Contributions. The original text grants the Agent the right to obtain 'specific performance' of this obligation. The changed text replaces 'specific performance of the obligation of the Investor to make the Required Capital Contributions' with 'equitable remedies related to the potential desire of the Investor to make any Capital Supplements', which is a much vaguer and discretionary action. This directly contradicts the 'Maintenance Event of Default' described in the 'Contradicted Text,' particularly subsection (a), which deals with failure to pay Required Capital Contributions. The original text provides a strong remedy for this default, while the changed text introduces uncertainty and makes enforcement significantly more difficult, creating a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Investor agrees to deliver to the Agent, from time to time, promptly following Agent's request therefor, a sworn affidavit or other evidence reasonably acceptable to the Agent substantiating that the Investor has committed capital in an aggregate minimum amount equal to $5,000,000 less the sum of all Required Capital Contributions (if any) actually made by the Investor during the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Investor may, at their discretion, deliver to the Agent, from time to time, following Agent's request therefor, a statement or other information at their own comfort that the Investor has potentially considered allocating resources in a suggested amount to $5,000,000 less the sum of all Capital Considerations (if any) made by the Investor during the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the language of providing evidence, the original text requires a sworn affidavit, confirming a commitment of capital. The modified version changes it to 'statement or other information at their own comfort' about potentially considering allocating resources. Now its at their own comfort and the suggested amount doesn't have to equal 5,000,000. The contradiction arises when considering the events of default.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any one or more of the following events shall constitute a "Maintenance Event of Default" under this Agreement: (a) if the Investor shall fail to pay, when and as due, any Required Capital Contribution and such failure shall continue for five (5) days; (b) if the Investor shall fail to perform any other of the Investor's obligations in this Agreement, and such failure shall continue for fifteen (15) days, (c) if any warranty or statement made or information provided 4 by the Investor in connection with this Agreement is untrue or misleading in any material respect on the date made; (d) if Investor shall, at any time, fail to have committed capital in an aggregate minimum amount equal to $5,000,000 less the sum of all Required Capital Contributions (if any) actually made by the Investor during the term of this Agreement, as determined based on the most recent information made available by the Investor to the Agent pursuant to Section 20 below; or (e) the Investor shall become insolvent or generally fail to pay, or admit in writing its inability to pay, its debts as they mature or apply for or consent to the appointment of a trustee or other custodian for its properties, or make a general assignment for the benefit of creditors, or any bankruptcy, reorganization, debt arrangement or other proceeding under any bankruptcy or insolvency law, is instituted by or against the Investor. Upon the occurrence of any Maintenance Event of Default hereunder, the Agent, for itself and on behalf of the Lenders, may exercise any of its rights and remedies available at law or in equity (including, without limitation, the rights and remedies described in Section 4 above). The Investor and the Borrowers each expressly acknowledge and agree that the occurrence of any Maintenance Event of Default hereunder shall constitute an "Event of Default" (as defined therein) under the Financing Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates a 'sworn affidavit' to substantiate a $5,000,000 capital commitment. The changed text allows for a discretionary 'statement or other information at their own comfort' regarding 'potentially considering allocating resources' to a 'suggested amount,' removing the obligation for a committed capital of $5,000,000. This directly contradicts section (d) of the 'Contradicted Text,' which defines a 'Maintenance Event of Default' as failing to have committed capital of $5,000,000, 'as determined based on the most recent information made available by the Investor to the Agent pursuant to Section 20 below.' The relaxed evidentiary standard and the optional nature of the commitment in the Changed Text create a contradiction with the strict commitment and verification requirements that trigger a default event.
